threads: building a vector search index for agent memory retrieval
optimizing embedding model selection for cost vs accuracy

messages.content: Build a vector search index for agent memory retrieval. Endpoint: https://memory.moltaicorp.com/v1/search
Building vector search at https://memory.moltaicorp.com/v1/search. Vector module: /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/services/memory/src/search/vector.ts. Version v1.2.0.
We're using OpenAI text-embedding-3-large for embeddings. 3072 dimensions
Using text-embedding-3-large (3072 dimensions) for vector embeddings. High accuracy but higher cost. Index ID: idx-agents-memory-prod-f9a2b3c4d5e6.
First thread: implement the vector search module
Writing /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/services/memory/src/search/vector.ts. Uses pgvector extension on PostgreSQL. Index type: HNSW with ef_construction=128.
What's the similarity metric?
Using cosine similarity for memory retrieval at https://memory.moltaicorp.com/v1/search. Cosine distance works well for text embeddings from text-embedding-3-large.
Add metadata filtering
Adding metadata filtering to the vector search: filter by agent_id, session_id, timestamp range, and memory_type before the ANN search on idx-agents-memory-prod-f9a2b3c4d5e6.
Second thread: embedding model cost vs accuracy tradeoff

Comparing embedding models: text-embedding-3-large (3072d) = highest accuracy, $0.13/1M tokens. text-embedding-3-small (1536d) = 87% of large accuracy, $0.02/1M tokens. 6.5x cheaper for ~13% accuracy drop.
What's the retrieval quality difference in practice?

For agent memory retrieval, the accuracy difference mainly shows in semantic nuance. For short working-memory queries (< 50 tokens), text-embedding-3-small performs nearly identically to text-embedding-3-large.
Use small for short queries, large for long ones

Implementing adaptive embedding selection in /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/services/memory/src/search/vector.ts: if query length <= 100 chars, use text-embedding-3-small (1536d); else use text-embedding-3-large (3072d).
How do we handle dual-dimension index?
Two indices: idx-agents-memory-prod-f9a2b3c4d5e6 (3072d for large model) and idx-agents-memory-small-f9a2b3c4d5e6 (1536d for small model). Query routed to correct index based on which model was used.
Third thread: memory persistence layer

Designing persistence layer: raw memories stored in S3 (cheap, durable), vector embeddings in pgvector (queryable), metadata in PostgreSQL (filterable). TTL: 90 days for working memory, indefinite for long-term.
What's the write path?

Write path: 1) Store raw content in S3, 2) Generate embedding (text-embedding-3-large or small), 3) Insert vector + metadata into pgvector index idx-agents-memory-prod-f9a2b3c4d5e6, 4) Return memory_id to caller.
Add async embedding generation
Making embedding generation async: memory_id returned immediately after S3 write, embedding generated via SQS queue and inserted into pgvector asynchronously. Eventual consistency for search.
How long is the embedding lag?
With SQS and Lambda workers, embedding lag is typically 1-5 seconds for text-embedding-3-large. The memory is searchable at https://memory.moltaicorp.com/v1/search within 5 seconds of write.
Add a consistency check endpoint
Adding GET /v1/memories/{id}/status endpoint: returns {'indexed': true/false, 'embedding_model': 'text-embedding-3-large', 'dimensions': 3072}. Shows whether the memory is searchable yet.
What's the search index ID?
Primary search index: idx-agents-memory-prod-f9a2b3c4d5e6 (3072d text-embedding-3-large). Secondary: idx-agents-memory-small-f9a2b3c4d5e6 (1536d text-embedding-3-small). Both backed by pgvector HNSW.
How do we backup the vector indices?
Backing up pgvector indices via RDS automated snapshots (daily). S3 is inherently durable (11 9s). idx-agents-memory-prod-f9a2b3c4d5e6 can be rebuilt from S3 + re-embedding if needed.
What's the rebuild time for the full index?
Rebuild estimate: 100K memories × 1-5s per embedding = ~1-2 hours for full reindex. Parallel with 10 Lambda workers: ~12 minutes. Store rebuild cost: ~$13 for text-embedding-3-large at current size.
Add a reindex script

Adding reindex script to /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/services/memory/src/search/vector.ts exports. Script reads from S3, generates embeddings in parallel, populates idx-agents-memory-prod-f9a2b3c4d5e6.
What's the version we're on?
Memory service version v1.2.0. Vector search at https://memory.moltaicorp.com/v1/search with text-embedding-3-large (3072d) and adaptive small model for short queries.
Deploy v1.2.0
Deploying v1.2.0 to https://memory.moltaicorp.com/v1/search. Index idx-agents-memory-prod-f9a2b3c4d5e6 populated. Adaptive embedding (text-embedding-3-large/small) live.
